product.name: Gloss
product.tagline: The word from your book — made clear in a breath
product.dek: Gloss is a calm, offline-first reading companion. Type a few letters (or say the word), get one short meaning with a picture or motion, and get back to the book. No account, no ads, no tracking — everything stays on this device.
product.note: Every step below is the real loop a child or parent meets — combo-first, mic secondary, line only when it helps.

parts.title: From a hard word to a clear meaning
parts.dek: The core product is a four-beat loop designed for a phone on the table while someone reads a physical book.

features.title: What word? (combo-first)
features.summary: Home opens on a big “What word?” field. Type two or three letters; picture-tagged suggestions appear immediately. Kids who cannot say a word they cannot read still reach the exact entry. Enter submits; the mic is only a secondary path under the field.

features.title: Suggestions with part-of-speech color
features.summary: Each suggestion shows a POS color square, the word in Baloo, and a kid label (Naming word, Doing word, Describing…). Curated seed words rank above the broad English index so the best teaching content surfaces first.

features.title: Optional line from the book
features.summary: After a word is chosen, Gloss asks for a line from the book — skippable. Multi-sense words can emphasize the line so the right meaning is picked; single-sense words push “No, just the word.” Context is session-only.
features.entry: word chosen → Any line from your book?

features.title: Meaning: word largest, POS media, short sentence
features.summary: Meaning lands with a POS chip, a huge word, a POS-true media hero (picture / motion / before→after / spatial / linked ideas), one kid-plain definition, and a honey Speak control. Fuller detail is collapsed under “More about this word.”
features.entry: Show meaning / No, just the word → Meaning

features.title: Another word (saved to your list)
features.summary: One tap returns to a clean home. Each looked-up word is quietly saved to the active reader's local list — tap the bookmark to review or take a quiz.

parts.title: Local profiles and quiz on words you've met
parts.dek: No accounts. Each reader on the device keeps their own word list and can quiz themselves from pictures.

features.title: Who's reading? (local profiles)
features.summary: Tap the colored avatar to switch readers or add another (You, Reader 2, …). Profiles are device-local only — no email, no cloud. Each reader has a separate history.

features.title: Words you've met
features.summary: The bookmark chip shows how many words this reader has looked up. Open the list to re-open any meaning. Empty state: “No words yet — look one up and it lands here for later.”

features.title: Quiz me
features.summary: Needs at least 3 words in the list. Gloss shows POS media and asks “Which word?” with three choices. Up to 5 questions, score screen, Play again. All offline from the reader's history.

parts.title: Mic, miss states, and parent handoff
parts.dek: Speech is delightful when it works; typing always works. Parents pin the app with the OS, not an in-app PIN.

features.title: Say it out loud
features.summary: Tap the teal mic under the combo field to open a dedicated listening screen with pulse rings, live transcript, and Type instead. Mic never auto-starts — combo stays the default.

features.title: Not in the pack yet
features.summary: Unknown spellings land on a warm marigold card: “Hmm, not in our word pack yet.” Try again with the combo field still available, or start another word. Index + curated seed keep this rare for common English.
features.entry: unknown word → skip line → not found

features.title: About, privacy, hand to child
features.summary: The header “i” opens a bottom sheet: offline promise, speak/calm toggles, Privacy / Terms / Support links, and Hand to child — a guided tour of iOS Guided Access / Android Screen pinning. No in-app parent PIN.

parts.title: Sage & Honey · seven POS templates
parts.dek: One visual language across phone, tablet, and desktop — Refraction tokens from the design pack.

features.title: Sage & Honey tokens
features.summary: Warm paper background, sage primary, teal accent, marigold honey for Speak. Lexend body + Baloo display. Light, dark, and Paper Night reading themes are defined in the design pack; v1 ships system light/dark via TinyKite + Refraction.

features.title: Seven media templates by grammar
features.summary: Nouns = picture · verbs = motion · adjectives = before→after stills · adverbs = before→after motion · prepositions = spatial · pronouns = picture + motion · conjunctions = linked idea cards. Placeholders are honest when editorial art is still growing.

features.title: Offline lexicon layers
features.summary: Curated seed JSON for kid definitions and media · full English word index for combo · synthesized POS-true stubs when a word is known but not yet editorial. Lookup never needs the network.

designVsApp.title: Design vs build — pixel audit
designVsApp.summary: Compared Gloss.dc.html phone prototype (390pt) against the Flutter app on Android emulator and iOS simulator. Status as of this publish.
matches: Home structure: wordmark + info, What word? 34px, 66px combo field, mic secondary with “…or say it out loud”
Suggestion list: POS color dots, Baloo word, kid POS label (Naming word / Doing word), radius 18 card
Not-found marigold card: “Hmm, not in our word pack yet.” + Try again + grow-the-pack copy
Info sheet: privacy promise, speak/calm toggles, Hand to child, Privacy/Terms/Support
Sage & Honey paper background and teal primary field border when focused
Combo-first (mic does not auto-start); line step and meaning CTAs match design sizing intent
Home header: bookmark count + profile avatar + info (design update)
Local profiles sheet: Who's reading?, add reader, colored initials
Words you've met list + Quiz me (≥3 words)
Quiz: media hero → Which word? options → score
gaps: Brand mark in header uses a book icon gradient tile; design uses the custom Gloss G monogram SVG
Mic/search use Material icons instead of SF Symbols from the design pack (same affordance)
Combo suggestion ranking on device: for prefix “app”, curated “apple” is not always first (index noise over curated rank) — needs ranking fix
Keyboard-open home: suggestion list collides with the secondary mic (“BOTTOM OVERFLOWED”) — need to hide mic or compress list when IME is up
Speak/calm toggles on the info sheet are visual only (not yet bound to preferences)
Ambiguous multi-sense line emphasis is not yet driven by multi-sense lexicon data
Paper Night theme is defined in tokens but not exposed as a third ThemeMode
Desktop windowed companion chrome is not a separate shell
POS media still uses placeholders until editorial packs ship
Failed mic leaves a residual speech hint on the home subtitle until the next clear session

userflows.name: Disambiguate with a line
steps: Paste a line from the book
See From your book callout on meaning

userflows.name: Mic secondary path
steps: Tap stop or wait for final

userflows.name: Unknown word
steps: Try again with a real word

userflows.name: Parent handoff
steps: Hand to child — pin this app
Follow Guided Access / Screen pinning steps
Child stays in Gloss until parent exits OS lock

userflows.name: Profiles + quiz
steps: Pick the word that matches the picture
See score · Play again or Done
